items.notes: Very common word; also means 'distance'. Used in many compounds like 'matkalaukku' (suitcase), 'matkalippu' (travel ticket).
examples: Matka kesti kaksi tuntia. — The trip lasted two hours.

items.notes: Compound word: 'matka' (trip) + 'laukku' (bag). Literally 'trip bag'.
examples: Pakkaa matkalaukku valmiiksi. — Pack the suitcase ready.

items.notes: Borrowed from Swedish/French. Finnish passports are issued by the police.
examples: Passini on voimassa viisi vuotta. — My passport is valid for five years.

items.notes: Finland is part of the Schengen area, so EU citizens don't need a visa. Stem: viisumi-, partitive: viisumia.
examples: Tarvitsetko viisumin Suomeen? — Do you need a visa to Finland?

items.notes: Compound word: 'lento' (flight) + 'lippu' (ticket). 'Lippu' alone means ticket and is in the transport category.
examples: Lentolippu Helsinkiin oli edullinen. — The plane ticket to Helsinki was affordable.

items.notes: From the verb 'varata' (to reserve). Stem: varaukse-, e.g. partitive: varausta. Used for hotel, restaurant, and travel bookings.
examples: Minulla on varaus hotellissa. — I have a reservation at the hotel.

items.notes: From the verb 'majoittaa' (to accommodate). Stem: majoitukse-, partitive: majoitusta. Covers hotels, hostels, cabins, etc.
examples: Majoitus sisältää aamiaisen. — The accommodation includes breakfast.

items.notes: Stem: reiti-, partitive: reittiä. Used for travel routes, bus routes, hiking trails, etc.
examples: Mikä on lyhin reitti keskustaan? — What is the shortest route to the center?

items.notes: Stem: osoittee-, partitive: osoitetta. Finnish addresses typically use street name + number format.
examples: Mikä on hotellin osoite? — What is the hotel's address?

items.notes: Stem: vasemma-. 'Käänny vasemmalle' means 'turn left' (allative case).
examples: Käänny vasemmalle liikennevaloissa. — Turn left at the traffic lights.

items.notes: Also means 'correct' or 'true'. Context makes the meaning clear. 'Käänny oikealle' means 'turn right'.
examples: Käänny oikealle seuraavasta risteyksestä. — Turn right at the next intersection.

items.notes: From the adjective 'suora' (straight). Very useful for directions.
examples: Mene suoraan kaksi korttelia. — Go straight ahead two blocks.

items.notes: Indicates direction of movement (toward the front). 'Edessä' means 'in front of' (static position). From stem ete-/ede-.
examples: Astu askel eteen. — Take a step forward.

items.notes: Indicates direction of movement (toward the back). 'Takana' means 'behind' (static position). From stem taka-.
examples: Katso taakse! — Look behind you!

items.notes: From 'lähi-' (near). Takes partitive: 'lähellä asemaa' (near the station).
examples: Kauppa on lähellä. — The shop is nearby.

items.notes: Opposite of 'lähellä'. Takes elative: 'kaukana keskustasta' (far from the center).
examples: Lentokenttä on kaukana keskustasta. — The airport is far from the center.

items.notes: Also used as an adjective meaning 'northern'. Stem: pohjoise-. Finland is one of the world's northernmost countries.
examples: Oulu on pohjoisen Suomen kaupunki. — Oulu is a city in northern Finland.

items.notes: Also used as an adjective meaning 'southern'. Stem: etelä-. 'Etelä-Suomi' means Southern Finland.
examples: Helsinki on Etelä-Suomessa. — Helsinki is in Southern Finland.

items.notes: Also used as an adjective meaning 'eastern'. Stem: idä-. 'Itä-Suomi' means Eastern Finland.
examples: Aurinko nousee idästä. — The sun rises from the east.

items.notes: Also used as an adjective meaning 'western'. Stem changes: länsi → länne- in inflected forms (e.g. 'lännestä' = from the west).
examples: Tuuli tulee lännestä. — The wind comes from the west.

items.notes: Stem: raja-. Finland shares borders with Sweden, Norway, and Russia. 'Rajalla' means 'at the border'.
examples: Suomen ja Ruotsin raja kulkee Tornionjokea pitkin. — The border between Finland and Sweden runs along the Tornio River.

items.notes: Be careful: 'tulli' (double l) = customs, but 'tuli' (single l) = fire. The double consonant changes the meaning completely!
examples: Menitkö tullin läpi? — Did you go through customs?

items.notes: Always plural. Compound: 'ulko' (outer) + 'maat' (countries/lands). Singular 'ulkomaa' means 'foreign country'. 'Ulkomailla' = abroad (location).
examples: Hän asuu ulkomailla. — He/she lives abroad.

items.notes: Stem changes: opas → oppaa- in inflected forms (e.g. 'oppaan' = guide's, 'oppaita' = guides). Can mean a person or a guidebook.
examples: Opas näytti meille kaupungin nähtävyydet. — The guide showed us the city's sights.

items.notes: From 'nähdä' (to see) + '-tävyys' (worthiness suffix). Literally 'something worth seeing'. Stem: nähtävyyde-, plural: nähtävyydet.
examples: Suomenlinna on Helsingin tunnetuin nähtävyys. — Suomenlinna is Helsinki's most famous attraction.

items.notes: Very common word with multiple meanings: country, land, ground, soil. Plural: 'maat'. 'Suomi on kaunis maa' = Finland is a beautiful country.
examples: Suomi on kaunis maa. — Finland is a beautiful country.

items.notes: Borrowed from English/French. The native Finnish equivalent 'matkailija' (traveler) is more formal. Stem: turisti-.
examples: Turistit vierailevat Suomenlinnassa. — Tourists visit Suomenlinna.
